General Policy Approach (Design Logic): Reduce catastrophic damage; build resilience; prepare for shocks
Specific Policy Options (Examples): Flood defences, sea walls, heatwave plans, emergency evacuation systems, backup infrastructure, nuclear safety systems

General Policy Approach (Design Logic): Reduce uncertainty where possible; otherwise prioritise robustness and flexibility
Specific Policy Options (Examples): Monitoring systems (climate, seismic), early warning systems, adaptive water management, flexible infrastructure planning, stress-testing systems

General Policy Approach (Design Logic): Apply precaution; limit exposure; avoid irreversible harm under deep uncertainty
Specific Policy Options (Examples): Restrict development in high-risk zones, strict safety standards, regulatory limits on new technologies, “no regrets” policies, safety margins in infrastructure

General Policy Approach (Design Logic): Prevent irreversible damage early; act at source; favour substitution and long-term system change
Specific Policy Options (Examples): Managed retreat from coasts, banning harmful substances, transitioning away from fossil fuels, ecosystem restoration, land-use change

General Policy Approach (Design Logic): Enable long-term action; overcome short-term incentives; institutionalise forward planning
Specific Policy Options (Examples): Carbon pricing, emissions caps, long-term infrastructure planning, climate adaptation strategies, international agreements (e.g. Paris Agreement)

Key Feature: Low risk but high public fear
General Policy Approach (Design Logic): Build trust; align perception with evidence; ensure legitimacy and participation
Specific Policy Options (Examples): Public engagement processes, transparent decision-making, risk communication campaigns, stakeholder consultations, participatory planning

Key Feature: “How sure are we about that estimate?”
